vocab.example: My very first botanical endeavor involved trying to grow cherry tomatoes in a small plastic pot on our windowsill.
Growing sunflowers in the backyard really helped to fuel my nascent interest in the natural world.
I was utterly captivated watching the mung beans germinate on a damp piece of cotton wool for a school project.

vocab.meaning: kiên cường, có sức sống mãnh liệt
vocab.example: I chose to grow aloe vera because it is incredibly resilient and could survive my occasional forgetfulness to water it.
I remember being mesmerized by the lush, vibrant foliage of the ferns that I kept in the shaded corner of our garden.
The most rewarding moment of my childhood was seeing the jasmine plant finally burst into bloom, filling the air with its scent.

vocab.meaning: tự duy trì, tự sống tốt
vocab.example: I preferred growing succulents as they were largely self-sustaining and required minimal intervention from me.
I was drawn to orchids not for their ease of care, but for the sheer aesthetic appeal of their intricate flowers.
I felt a pang of sadness when my first marigold plant began to shrivel up due to an unexpected heatwave.
After months of careful tending, we were able to reap the benefits by using our home-grown herbs in every family meal.
